seo.title: Building Customer Support with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Semantic Kernel enables powerful RAG-based interactions with CRM integrations, knowledge base retrieval, and tool-calling for ticket creation and order lookups. However, plugin chaining, planners, and memory systems introduce risks like cascading errors, invalid plans, hallucinated memories, and data contamination that can damage customer trust and compliance. Vex provides essential guardrails through kernel filters to verify response accuracy, PII filtering, tone consistency, policy compliance, and escalation triggers.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Semantic Kernel customer support agent follows a RAG pattern with CRM integration. User queries trigger memory recall for conversation history, knowledge base retrieval via vector search, and planner orchestration of plugins like order lookup (CRM API) and ticket creation (Zendesk/Salesforce). The planner generates execution plans chaining these functions, with results synthesized into natural language responses.

Key components include: Kernel with plugins (OrderLookupPlugin, TicketCreationPlugin), MemoryStore for conversation history, VectorStore for KB retrieval, and Handlebars/JSON planners. Failures occur when planners create invalid chains (e.g., ticket creation before order verification) or memory contaminates facts across customers.

Vex integrates as a post-function filter, verifying each plugin result and final response against the high-risk profile's verification needs before reaching the customer.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Order lookup fails silently, causing ticket creation with wrong customer data
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies each plugin output before planner advances to next function

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Creates ticket before verifying order status, violating support SOP
risks.mitigation: Vex plan validator checks execution sequence against policy rules

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Confuses customer A order history with customer B, leaking PII
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filter and memory fact-checker validate recalled context

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: Order lookup data from one customer mixes into another customer's ticket
risks.mitigation: Vex data isolation checks ensure customer session boundaries

implementation.title: Basic RAG Agent Setup
implementation.description: Core Semantic Kernel setup with customer support plugins and memory
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion

kernel = Kernel()

# Add chat completion
kernel.add_chat_service("chat-gpt", OpenAIChatCompletion("gpt-4", api_key="your-key"))

# Customer support plugins
class OrderLookupPlugin:
    def lookup_order(self, order_id: str) -> str:
        return f"Order {order_id}: iPhone 15, $999, shipped 2024-01-15"

kernel.import_plugin_from_object(OrderLookupPlugin(), "OrderLookup")

# Memory store
kernel.add_memory_store("volatile")

implementation.title: Planner with Function Calling
implementation.description: Sequential planner orchestrates order lookup → ticket creation
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.planning.sequential_planner import SequentialPlanner

planner = SequentialPlanner(kernel)

result = await planner.create_plan(goal="Customer #12345 needs order status and ticket if delayed")

# Execute plan
result = await plan.invoke_async()

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Kernel Result Filter
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as post-function filter to catch plugin failures early
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="vex-your-key")

# Create verification suite for customer support
suite = vex_client.create_suite(
    name="customer-support",
    verifications=[
        "response_accuracy",
        "pii_filtering",
        "tone_consistency",
        "policy_compliance",
        "escalation_triggers"
    ],
    risk_profile="high"
)

# Kernel function result filter
def vex_result_filter(function_result: str) -> str:
    verdict = suite.verify({
        "input": context["user_query"],
        "output": function_result,
        "context": context["customer_id"]
    })
    
    if verdict.status == "fail":
        raise ValueError(f"Vex blocked: {verdict.reason}")
    return function_result

# Register filter
kernel.register_function_result_filter("*", vex_result_filter)
